records.stage_evidence: Diagnostic ultrasound report with findings and opinion

ocr.verbatim:
PT'S NAME: 
REF BY Dr. 
LIVER: Liver is normal in size & Parenchymal echotexture homogenous. 
Intrahepatic biliary radicals are not dilated. 
No mass lesion seen. 
G.B: A calculus size 16.5mm seen in the lumen of GB with acoustic shadowing. 
Wall thickened 3.2mm. No mass lesion seen 
CBD: Normal in caliber. 
PANCREAS: No calcification, no mass lesion seen. MPD not dilated 
SPLEEN: Normal in size with normal echotexture. 
RT.KIDNEY: Size & echotexture normal. 
No calculus, no mass lesion seen. 
RT ureter is not dilated. 
LT.KIDNEY: Size & echotexture normal. 
No calculus, no mass lesion seen. 
LT Ureter is not dilated. 
U.B.: Normal wall. No calculus. No mass seen. 
UTERUS: Atrophied. 
Both ovaries are not visualized. 
OPINION: CHOLECYSTITIS WITH CHOLELETHIASIS. 
अत्याधुनिक मशीनों द्वारा उपलब्ध सुविधाएं : 
Pathology, Ultrasound, Digital X-Ray, Electrolyte & All Special Test 
Timing : 8:30 am. to 7:30 pm. 
Not for Medicolegal Purpose

Not for Medicolegal Purpose

records.summary: This is a typed ultrasound report detailing findings from an abdominal ultrasound. It describes normal liver, pancreas, spleen, and kidneys, but identifies a 16.5mm calculus in the gallbladder with wall thickening. The report concludes with an opinion of cholecystitis with cholelithiasis.
key_observations: 16.5mm calculus in gallbladder with acoustic shadowing and thickened wall (3.2mm)
Normal liver, pancreas, spleen, and kidneys

records.stage_evidence: No catheters, stents, or contrast agents visible; diagnostic imaging only

records.summary: Two ultrasound images show the left and right kidneys and surrounding structures. The top image shows the left kidney and spleen. The bottom image shows the right kidney and common bile duct (CBD).
key_observations: Left kidney (LT KIDNEY) and spleen visible in top image
Right kidney (RT KIDNEY) and common bile duct (CBD) visible in bottom image
Scan date: 11/03/2026 (likely a placeholder or error, as it's in the future)

records.summary: Two ultrasound images from a single exam showing the pancreas and gallbladder with a calculus (GB CAL), and the uterus and liver. The scan was performed on March 11, 2026.
key_observations: Pancreas is visualized with a measurement of 1.65 cm.
Uterus and liver are also visualized.
